---
title: "The CLI: quartobot"
---

::: {.callout-warning}
The CLI doesn't exist yet. This page describes the design as it lands.
Tracking issues: [#22](https://github.com/seandavi/quartobot/issues/22)
(package scaffold + `scan`), [#25](https://github.com/seandavi/quartobot/issues/25)
(`init` and other scaffolding commands).
:::

A Python CLI for pre-render and out-of-render work that
`pandoc-manubot-cite` doesn't do (or doesn't do ergonomically). Plus
`usethis`-style scaffolding into existing Quarto projects.

```bash
pip install quartobot
```

`quartobot` depends on `manubot`, so this also puts `pandoc-manubot-cite`
on `PATH`.

## Pre-render commands

### `quartobot scan <path>`

Walk `.qmd`/`.md` under a path, list every persistent-identifier cite
key, group by prefix, surface duplicates. Pure read. No network.

```
$ quartobot scan .
@doi:
  10.1371/journal.pcbi.1007128 (3 references)
@pmid:
  31479462 (1 reference)
@quarto2024 (1 reference, hand-curated â€” see references.bib)
```

Useful in code review and as a pre-commit hook.

### `quartobot resolve [<keys>...]`

Pre-fetch citations via `manubot.cite` and write to `references.json`
(and optionally append BibTeX to `references.bib`). The point isn't to
replace what manubot does at render â€” it's to do it on a developer's
machine before push, so CI never sees a Crossref hiccup.

```
$ quartobot resolve --from-scan .
Resolving 2 keys...
  doi:10.1371/journal.pcbi.1007128  âś“
  pmid:31479462                      âś“
Wrote references.json (2 entries)
Updated _freeze/manubot-cache.json
```

### `quartobot validate <project>`

Pre-flight / CI-lint surface. Every key in the prose resolves cleanly;
`_quarto.yml` declares both bibliographies; `manubot-bibliography-cache`
is set. Exit nonzero on any failure.

```
$ quartobot validate .
âś— @doi:10.1371/nonexistent â€” unresolved (Crossref 404)
âś— _quarto.yml missing manubot-bibliography-cache
2 issues. Exit 1.
```

## Scaffolding commands

### `quartobot init`

In an existing Quarto project, add the citation extension, merge the
manubot keys into `_quarto.yml`, seed `references.bib`, drop in the
version banner template, write the ten-line `render.yml` wrapper.
Idempotent.

### `quartobot use-render-workflow`

Just the workflow file.

### `quartobot use-banner`

Just the banner template + the `include-before-body` line in
`_quarto.yml`.

### `quartobot use-quarto-yml`

The `_quarto.yml` manubot block (filters, bibliography, `manubot-*`
keys).

### `quartobot detach`

Convert the ten-line reusable-workflow wrapper into the full pipeline
copied out. For users who want to fork the pipeline. After `detach`,
upstream fixes don't flow through.

## Philosophy

Opaque-by-default (reusable workflow): user gets a ten-line `render.yml`
and benefits from upstream fixes. `detach` is the escape hatch when they
outgrow that â€” they own the full workflow forever after.

The opposite of `r-lib/actions`, which copies 150 lines into every
consumer repo. quartobot's default is friendlier; the escape hatch
matches their model for users who want it.
